name: extended-eval-corpus-v1
description: Offline corpus built from the real generated OneNote pages (54 pages + 4 readable .md attachments) by eval/build_extended_dataset.py.

documents.title: First Week Plan for New Engineers
documents.section_path: Onboarding / First Week Plan for New Engineers
documents.chunk_text: What a new software engineer should accomplish in the first five working days. Day 1 is laptop handover and account activation; accounts are provisioned within 4 business hours of the IT ticket. Every new engineer is paired with an onboarding buddy for the first 30 days. By end of week one the new hire ships one small change to the internal sandbox repository. Related terms: new hire onboarding, first days at work, getting started guide, what to expect in your first days, expectations for a new developer, assigned buddy and first tasks when you join.

documents.title: First Week Plan for New Engineers
documents.section_path: Onboarding / First Week Plan for New Engineers
documents.chunk_text: Mandatory security awareness training must be completed within the first 5 days. The onboarding buddy books a 30-minute daily check-in for the first week.

documents.title: First Week Plan for New Engineers
documents.section_path: Onboarding / First Week Plan for New Engineers
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Collect the laptop and sign the asset handover form from IT. Activate SSO and enroll a second MFA factor before opening any tool. Clone the sandbox repository and run the bootstrap script. Open the first starter ticket tagged 'good-first-issue' with your buddy. Complete security awareness training and confirm to your manager.

documents.title: Developer Workstation Setup
documents.section_path: Onboarding / Developer Workstation Setup
documents.chunk_text: Baseline tools every engineer installs before joining a project. The standard toolchain is Git, Docker Desktop, Node.js 20 LTS, Python 3.12, and the JetBrains or VS Code IDE. Company laptops ship with full-disk encryption enabled; never disable it. The internal package registry is at registry.northwind.local and requires an SSO token. Related terms: dev environment setup, developer machine, tools installation, programs to install before you start coding, required software and toolchain, what to put on your machine.

documents.title: Developer Workstation Setup
documents.section_path: Onboarding / Developer Workstation Setup
documents.chunk_text: Git commits must be signed; the GPG key is registered in GitHub during setup. WSL2 with Ubuntu 22.04 is the supported Linux environment on Windows laptops.

documents.title: Developer Workstation Setup
documents.section_path: Onboarding / Developer Workstation Setup
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Install Git, Docker Desktop, Node.js 20, and Python 3.12. Authenticate to the internal registry with 'nw login'. Generate and register a signing key for Git. Run the workstation doctor to verify the setup.

documents.title: Accounts and Access on Day One
documents.section_path: Onboarding / Accounts and Access on Day One
documents.chunk_text: Which accounts a new hire receives automatically and which require a request. SSO, email, Slack, and GitHub are provisioned automatically from the HR start record. Production and customer-data access is never granted on day one and requires manager + security approval. Access requests are submitted in the Access Portal and approved within 1 business day. Related terms: initial accounts, user provisioning, login access, which logins work on your first sign-in, accounts ready when you start, single sign-on accounts provisioned automatically.

documents.title: Accounts and Access on Day One
documents.section_path: Onboarding / Accounts and Access on Day One
documents.chunk_text: Contractors receive time-boxed accounts that expire on their contract end date.

documents.title: Accounts and Access on Day One
documents.section_path: Onboarding / Accounts and Access on Day One
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Confirm SSO, email, Slack, and GitHub work after first login. Request team-specific tools through the Access Portal. Ask your manager to approve any elevated access.

documents.title: Team Rituals and Cadence
documents.section_path: Onboarding / Team Rituals and Cadence
documents.chunk_text: Recurring meetings and planning cycles a new hire should expect. Teams run two-week sprints starting on Wednesdays. Daily standup is 15 minutes at 10:00 in the team channel huddle. Sprint review and retro happen on the second Tuesday afternoon. Related terms: meeting schedule, standup time, sprint ceremonies.

documents.title: Team Rituals and Cadence
documents.section_path: Onboarding / Team Rituals and Cadence
documents.chunk_text: Incident reviews are blameless and scheduled within 3 business days of resolution.

documents.title: Team Rituals and Cadence
documents.section_path: Onboarding / Team Rituals and Cadence
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Add the team ceremony calendar to your schedule. Post your standup update before 10:00 if you cannot attend live. Bring one improvement idea to the retro.

documents.title: First Month Milestones
documents.section_path: Onboarding / First Month Milestones
documents.chunk_text: What 'ramped up' means by the end of the first month. By day 30 the engineer has merged at least five pull requests. By day 30 the engineer can deploy to staging without help. A 30-day manager review confirms readiness for on-call shadowing. Related terms: 30 day plan, ramp up goals, early milestones.

documents.title: First Month Milestones
documents.section_path: Onboarding / First Month Milestones
documents.chunk_text: On-call shadowing starts only after the engineer has completed the runbook walkthrough.

documents.title: First Month Milestones
documents.section_path: Onboarding / First Month Milestones
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Track milestones with your manager in the 1:1 document. Schedule the 30-day review in week four. Begin on-call shadowing after the review.

documents.title: Onboarding Buddy Responsibilities
documents.section_path: Onboarding / Onboarding Buddy Responsibilities
documents.chunk_text: What is expected from an assigned onboarding buddy. The buddy is a peer engineer, not the new hire's manager. Buddies commit roughly 3 hours per week for the first two weeks. The buddy reviews the new hire's first pull request within the same day. Related terms: mentor, onboarding partner, peer support.

documents.title: Onboarding Buddy Responsibilities
documents.section_path: Onboarding / Onboarding Buddy Responsibilities
documents.chunk_text: Buddy assignments rotate so no engineer buddies more than once per quarter.

documents.title: Onboarding Buddy Responsibilities
documents.section_path: Onboarding / Onboarding Buddy Responsibilities
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Introduce the new hire to the team and key contacts. Pair on the first starter ticket. Review the first pull request promptly.

documents.title: Git Branching and Pull Request Workflow
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Git Branching and Pull Request Workflow
documents.chunk_text: The trunk-based branching model and pull request rules every repo follows. We use trunk-based development: short-lived branches merged into main, no long-running release branches. Pull requests require one approval, and two approvals for changes touching auth or billing. Branch names follow the pattern type/ticket-id-short-description, e.g. feat/NW-1234-add-export. Related terms: version control workflow, merge rules, branch policy.

documents.title: Git Branching and Pull Request Workflow
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Git Branching and Pull Request Workflow
documents.chunk_text: main is protected: direct pushes are blocked and CI must pass before merge. Stale branches with no activity for 30 days are deleted automatically.

documents.title: Git Branching and Pull Request Workflow
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Git Branching and Pull Request Workflow
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Branch from main with a ticket-prefixed name. Open a pull request early as a draft. Get the required approvals and a green CI run. Squash-merge into main.

documents.title: Code Review Standards
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Code Review Standards
documents.chunk_text: What reviewers check and the expected turnaround. Reviewers respond within one business day; if unavailable, reassign. Reviews focus on correctness, tests, readability, and security, in that order. A pull request over 400 changed lines should be split unless it is generated code. Related terms: pull request review, review feedback, review turnaround.

documents.title: Code Review Standards
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Code Review Standards
documents.chunk_text: Authors must respond to every review comment before merge. Approving a PR means you share responsibility for the change.

documents.title: Code Review Standards
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Code Review Standards
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Read the ticket and PR description before the diff. Check tests exist and cover the change. Leave actionable comments, not vague ones. Approve only when you would be comfortable owning the change.

documents.title: Coding Standards and Formatting
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Coding Standards and Formatting
documents.chunk_text: Language formatters and linters enforced in CI. Python uses ruff and black; TypeScript uses ESLint and Prettier. Formatting is enforced in CI and a failing format check blocks merge. Maximum line length is 100 characters for Python and 100 for TypeScript. Related terms: linting rules, code style, formatting policy.

documents.title: Coding Standards and Formatting
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Coding Standards and Formatting
documents.chunk_text: Public functions require docstrings or JSDoc describing parameters and return values. Type checking runs with mypy (strict) and tsc (strict) on every PR.

documents.title: Coding Standards and Formatting
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Coding Standards and Formatting
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Install the pre-commit hooks with 'pre-commit install'. Run formatters locally before pushing. Fix lint and type errors rather than suppressing them.

documents.title: Architecture Decision Records
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Architecture Decision Records
documents.chunk_text: How and when to write an ADR. ADRs live in docs/adr/ in each repository and are numbered sequentially. Write an ADR for any decision that is costly to reverse, such as a database or framework choice. An ADR has four parts: context, decision, status, and consequences. Related terms: architecture decision record, design decision documentation.

documents.title: Architecture Decision Records
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Architecture Decision Records
documents.chunk_text: ADRs are immutable once accepted; a new ADR supersedes an old one.

documents.title: Architecture Decision Records
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Architecture Decision Records
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Copy the ADR template into docs/adr/. Fill in context, decision, and consequences. Open a PR and get architecture review. Mark the ADR accepted when merged.

documents.title: Testing Strategy
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Testing Strategy
documents.chunk_text: The testing pyramid and coverage expectations. Target coverage is 80% lines for changed code, enforced on the diff, not the whole repo. Unit tests run on every PR; integration tests run on merge to main. End-to-end tests run nightly against staging. Related terms: test coverage, quality assurance strategy, unit and integration testing.

documents.title: Testing Strategy
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Testing Strategy
documents.chunk_text: Flaky tests are quarantined within 24 hours and fixed within a week.

documents.title: Testing Strategy
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Testing Strategy
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Write unit tests alongside the code. Add an integration test for any new external dependency. Keep tests deterministic and independent.

documents.title: Naming Conventions
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Naming Conventions
documents.chunk_text: Naming rules for repositories, services, and environment variables. Repositories use kebab-case, e.g. billing-service. Kubernetes services use the pattern <team>-<service>, e.g. payments-billing. Environment variables are UPPER_SNAKE_CASE and prefixed by component. Related terms: naming rules, identifier conventions, feature flag naming format, how to name a feature flag.

documents.title: Naming Conventions
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Naming Conventions
documents.chunk_text: Feature flags use the pattern area.flag-name, e.g. checkout.new-cart.

documents.title: Naming Conventions
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / Naming Conventions
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Check the catalog before naming something new. Propose additions via a PR to the naming catalog.

documents.title: Billing Service Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Billing Service Setup
documents.chunk_text: Local setup for the billing-service backend (Python/FastAPI). billing-service is a FastAPI app backed by PostgreSQL and Stripe. It runs locally on port 8020 and uses a seeded test database. Stripe test keys are pulled from the Secrets Vault path secret/billing/stripe-test. Related terms: payments service, billing backend local run, stripe test credentials for local development, payment provider test keys.

documents.title: Billing Service Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Billing Service Setup
documents.chunk_text: The smoke test creates a test invoice and asserts a paid webhook is received.

documents.title: Billing Service Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Billing Service Setup
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Clone the repo and create a virtualenv. Pull Stripe test secrets from the vault. Start dependencies with docker compose. Run migrations and the smoke test.

documents.title: Web App Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Web App Setup
documents.chunk_text: Local setup for the customer web app (React/TypeScript/Vite). web-app is a React 19 + TypeScript app built with Vite. It runs on port 5173 and proxies the API gateway at api.dev.northwind.local. Component tests use Vitest; end-to-end tests use Playwright. Related terms: frontend setup, customer portal application.

documents.title: Web App Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Web App Setup
documents.chunk_text: Design tokens come from the shared @northwind/ui package.

documents.title: Web App Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Web App Setup
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Clone the repo and install dependencies with npm. Copy the example env file. Start the dev server and run the tests.

documents.title: Search Service Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Search Service Setup
documents.chunk_text: Local setup for the search-service (Go) backed by OpenSearch. search-service is written in Go and indexes documents into OpenSearch. It runs on port 8040 and depends on a local OpenSearch container. The reindex command rebuilds the index from the catalog database. Related terms: search backend, indexing service.

documents.title: Search Service Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Search Service Setup
documents.chunk_text: Health is exposed at /healthz and readiness at /readyz.

documents.title: Search Service Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Search Service Setup
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Clone the repo and start OpenSearch with docker compose. Build the service with make. Run the reindex and the smoke test.

documents.title: Data Pipeline Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Data Pipeline Setup
documents.chunk_text: Local setup for the analytics data pipeline (Python/Airflow). The pipeline orchestrates daily ETL jobs with Apache Airflow. Airflow runs locally on port 8080 with the example DAGs disabled. Source data is mocked from data/fixtures during local runs. Related terms: ETL jobs, analytics workflow.

documents.title: Data Pipeline Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Data Pipeline Setup
documents.chunk_text: The dbt models build into a local DuckDB file for fast iteration.

documents.title: Data Pipeline Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Data Pipeline Setup
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Start Airflow with the provided docker compose file. Seed local fixtures. Trigger the daily_etl DAG and confirm success.

documents.title: Mobile App Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Mobile App Setup
documents.chunk_text: Local setup for the Northwind mobile app (Flutter). The mobile app is built with Flutter and targets iOS and Android. Flutter 3.24 is the supported version; mismatched versions fail CI. The app points at the staging API by default via --dart-define. Related terms: smartphone application, iOS and Android build.

documents.title: Mobile App Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Mobile App Setup
documents.chunk_text: Golden tests verify key screens render pixel-consistently.

documents.title: Mobile App Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Mobile App Setup
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Install Flutter 3.24 and run flutter doctor. Fetch packages and run the analyzer. Run the app against staging and execute tests.

documents.title: Internal CLI Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Internal CLI Setup
documents.chunk_text: Installing the 'nw' internal command-line tool. The nw CLI bootstraps projects, reads vault secrets, and runs environment checks. It is installed from the internal PyPI mirror and updated weekly. nw doctor validates Git, Docker, registry auth, and vault access. Related terms: internal command line tool, developer CLI.

documents.title: Internal CLI Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Internal CLI Setup
documents.chunk_text: nw is required by most other project setup pages.

documents.title: Internal CLI Setup
documents.section_path: Project Setups / Internal CLI Setup
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Install nw from the internal mirror. Authenticate with SSO. Run nw doctor and resolve any red checks.

documents.title: Production Release Process
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Production Release Process
documents.chunk_text: How a change goes from merged to deployed in production. Production deploys happen via the CD pipeline after a green staging soak of at least 2 hours. Deploys are blocked during the Friday 16:00 to Monday 09:00 freeze unless it is a hotfix. Every release names a rollback owner before rollout starts. Related terms: production deployment, shipping to production, go live process.

documents.title: Production Release Process
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Production Release Process
documents.chunk_text: Canary rollout sends 5% of traffic for 15 minutes before full rollout.

documents.title: Production Release Process
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Production Release Process
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Confirm staging soak passed and approvals are recorded. Start the canary at 5% and watch error rate and latency. Promote to 100% if the canary is clean. Record the release note and monitoring link.

documents.title: Rollback Procedure
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Rollback Procedure
documents.chunk_text: How to roll back a bad production release quickly. Rollback is a one-command redeploy of the previous known-good image tag. The rollback target is always the image tagged last-stable. Database migrations are backward compatible for at least one release to make rollback safe. Related terms: revert a release, restore previous version, undo a deployment.

documents.title: Rollback Procedure
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Rollback Procedure
documents.chunk_text: If a migration is not reversible, a forward-fix is used instead of rollback.

documents.title: Rollback Procedure
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Rollback Procedure
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Declare a rollback in the incident channel. Run the rollback command to last-stable. Confirm error rate returns to baseline. Open a follow-up to forward-fix the root cause.

documents.title: Feature Flag Management
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Feature Flag Management
documents.chunk_text: How features are shipped dark and enabled gradually. Flags are managed in the Feature Flag Console and default to off in production. Every flag has an owner and an expiry date; expired flags are reported weekly. Risky flags are enabled by cohort: internal users, then 10%, then 100%. Related terms: feature toggles, gradual rollout, dark launch.

documents.title: Feature Flag Management
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Feature Flag Management
documents.chunk_text: A flag must be removed within 60 days of reaching 100%.

documents.title: Feature Flag Management
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Feature Flag Management
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Create the flag with an owner and expiry. Enable for internal users first. Ramp by cohort while watching metrics. Remove the flag after full rollout.

documents.title: Staging Environment Policy
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Staging Environment Policy
documents.chunk_text: What staging is for and how it stays trustworthy. Staging mirrors production configuration and is refreshed with anonymized data weekly. Staging is shared; do not run destructive load tests without booking a window. A change must pass staging before it is eligible for production. Related terms: pre-production environment, test environment policy.

documents.title: Staging Environment Policy
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Staging Environment Policy
documents.chunk_text: Staging uses real third-party sandboxes, not mocks.

documents.title: Staging Environment Policy
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Staging Environment Policy
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Deploy your change to staging via the pipeline. Run the relevant smoke and integration checks. Leave staging in a clean state for the next team.

documents.title: Release Notes and Changelog
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Release Notes and Changelog
documents.chunk_text: How customer-facing release notes are produced. Customer release notes are generated from PR titles labeled 'changelog'. Notes are published every second Wednesday with the sprint release. Breaking changes require a migration note and 30 days notice to customers. Related terms: changelog, customer announcements.

documents.title: Release Notes and Changelog
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Release Notes and Changelog
documents.chunk_text: Internal-only changes are excluded from the public changelog.

documents.title: Release Notes and Changelog
documents.section_path: Releases and Deployment / Release Notes and Changelog
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Label customer-facing PRs with 'changelog'. Review the generated draft before publishing. Add migration notes for breaking changes.

documents.title: High API Latency Runbook
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / High API Latency Runbook
documents.chunk_text: Diagnosing and mitigating elevated API gateway latency. The page latency alert fires when p95 exceeds 800 ms for 5 minutes. The most common cause is database connection pool exhaustion. Check the Grafana 'API Overview' dashboard first, panel 'p95 by route'. Related terms: slow API, response time issues, performance incident.

documents.title: High API Latency Runbook
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / High API Latency Runbook
documents.chunk_text: Scaling the API deployment to 6 replicas is the standard first mitigation.

documents.title: High API Latency Runbook
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / High API Latency Runbook
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Acknowledge the PagerDuty alert. Open the API Overview dashboard and find the slow route. Check the database connection pool saturation panel. Scale replicas or shed load, then verify p95 recovers.

documents.title: Failed Deployment Runbook
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / Failed Deployment Runbook
documents.chunk_text: What to do when a production deployment fails health checks. The pipeline automatically halts rollout if readiness checks fail for 3 minutes. A failed deploy never takes down the running version; old pods keep serving. Most failures are missing config or a failed migration. Related terms: broken deployment, failed rollout recovery.

documents.title: Failed Deployment Runbook
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / Failed Deployment Runbook
documents.chunk_text: If unsure, roll back to last-stable rather than debugging in production.

documents.title: Failed Deployment Runbook
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / Failed Deployment Runbook
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Read the pipeline logs for the failing step. Check for missing config or migration errors. Roll back to last-stable if the fix is not obvious. Open an incident if customers were affected.

documents.title: Database Connection Errors
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / Database Connection Errors
documents.chunk_text: Resolving 'too many connections' and timeout errors from PostgreSQL. The primary database max_connections is 400; pgbouncer pools at 1000 client connections. A connection leak usually shows as steadily rising idle-in-transaction sessions. Restarting the leaking service clears leaked connections immediately. Related terms: database errors, connection pool exhausted, postgres timeouts.

documents.title: Database Connection Errors
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / Database Connection Errors
documents.chunk_text: Long-running queries over 30 seconds are killed by a watchdog.

documents.title: Database Connection Errors
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / Database Connection Errors
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Query pg_stat_activity for idle-in-transaction sessions. Identify the owning service. Restart that service to clear leaks. File a bug to fix the leak at the source.

documents.title: Docker Compose Port Conflict
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / Docker Compose Port Conflict
documents.chunk_text: Fixing local 'port is already allocated' errors. The error means another process or a stale container holds the port. Common conflicts are 5432 (Postgres), 6379 (Redis), and 8080 (app). Removing orphaned containers usually frees the port. Related terms: port already in use, address in use error, local container conflict.

documents.title: Docker Compose Port Conflict
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / Docker Compose Port Conflict
documents.chunk_text: Changing the host port mapping in an override file avoids the conflict entirely.

documents.title: Docker Compose Port Conflict
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / Docker Compose Port Conflict
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Find what holds the port. Stop the stale container or process. Re-run docker compose up.

documents.title: Vector Search Returns No Results
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / Vector Search Returns No Results
documents.chunk_text: Why semantic search may return nothing and how to fix it. An empty result set usually means the embedding model or vector dimension changed without a reindex. The collection vector size must match the embedding model dimension exactly. Recreating the collection requires a full reindex of all documents. Related terms: semantic search empty results, embedding index problem.

documents.title: Vector Search Returns No Results
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / Vector Search Returns No Results
documents.chunk_text: Check the collection info endpoint to confirm dimension and point count.

documents.title: Vector Search Returns No Results
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / Vector Search Returns No Results
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Check the collection dimension and point count. Compare against the configured embedding model dimension. Recreate the collection and reindex if they differ.

documents.title: GitHub Access and Teams
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / GitHub Access and Teams
documents.chunk_text: How repository access maps to GitHub teams. Access is granted through GitHub teams, never to individuals directly. Each repository has a CODEOWNERS file that defines required reviewers. Write access to infra repositories requires security approval. Related terms: repository permissions, repo access request, code access, adding an individual personal account to a repo, granting repository access to a person.

documents.title: GitHub Access and Teams
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / GitHub Access and Teams
documents.chunk_text: Outside collaborators are reviewed every quarter.

documents.title: GitHub Access and Teams
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / GitHub Access and Teams
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Request to join the relevant GitHub team in the Access Portal. Wait for the team owner to approve. Verify you can clone and open a draft PR.

documents.title: Secrets Vault Usage
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / Secrets Vault Usage
documents.chunk_text: Reading and rotating secrets safely. Secrets live in HashiCorp Vault under per-team paths like secret/<team>/<app>. Secrets are never committed to Git; CI reads them at deploy time. Production secrets rotate every 90 days automatically. Related terms: credentials storage, passwords and keys, secret management, storing a password in the repository, committing credentials to git, keeping secrets out of source control.

documents.title: Secrets Vault Usage
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / Secrets Vault Usage
documents.chunk_text: Reading a production secret is audited and may trigger a review.

documents.title: Secrets Vault Usage
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / Secrets Vault Usage
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Authenticate to Vault with your SSO token. Read only the path your service needs. Never paste secrets into chat or tickets.

documents.title: CI Pipeline Access and Reruns
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / CI Pipeline Access and Reruns
documents.chunk_text: How to view, rerun, and debug CI jobs. CI runs on GitHub Actions with self-hosted runners labeled nw-linux and nw-macos. Anyone on the repo can rerun a failed job; only owners can edit workflows. A job stuck in queue for over 10 minutes usually means no runner is available. Related terms: build pipeline, continuous integration jobs, rerunning failed builds.

documents.title: CI Pipeline Access and Reruns
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / CI Pipeline Access and Reruns
documents.chunk_text: Build artifacts are retained for 14 days.

documents.title: CI Pipeline Access and Reruns
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / CI Pipeline Access and Reruns
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Open the Actions tab on the repository. Rerun the failed job or download logs. Escalate to Build Infrastructure if no runner picks it up.

documents.title: Observability Stack Access
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / Observability Stack Access
documents.chunk_text: Getting into Grafana, logs, and traces. Dashboards are in Grafana at grafana.northwind.local with SSO. Logs are in Loki and queried through Grafana Explore. Traces are in Tempo, linked from each service dashboard. Related terms: monitoring dashboards, logs and traces, metrics access, distributed tracing tool, following a request across services, request traces.

documents.title: Observability Stack Access
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / Observability Stack Access
documents.chunk_text: Alert routing is configured per team in the Alerting section.

documents.title: Observability Stack Access
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / Observability Stack Access
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Sign in to Grafana with SSO. Find your team folder of dashboards. Use Explore for ad-hoc log and trace queries.

documents.title: Jira Projects and Boards
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / Jira Projects and Boards
documents.chunk_text: Finding the right board and requesting access. Each team has a Jira project keyed by a short code, e.g. NW for the platform team. Tickets follow the workflow Backlog, In Progress, In Review, Done. Access is requested through the Access Portal and approved by the project lead. Related terms: task tracker, ticket board, issue tracking.

documents.title: Jira Projects and Boards
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / Jira Projects and Boards
documents.chunk_text: Cross-team work uses linked issues rather than moving tickets between projects.

documents.title: Jira Projects and Boards
documents.section_path: Internal Tools and Access / Jira Projects and Boards
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Request access to the project in the Access Portal. Use the team board filter for your sprint. Link cross-team dependencies instead of copying tickets.

documents.title: VPN Access and Recovery
documents.section_path: IT Support / VPN Access and Recovery
documents.chunk_text: Connecting to the corporate VPN and recovering lost access. The VPN client is Cisco AnyConnect pointing at vpn.northwind.local. VPN requires SSO plus a push approval on your enrolled MFA device. If the certificate is invalid, reinstall the profile from the Self-Service Portal. Related terms: remote network access, corporate tunnel, connection recovery.

documents.title: VPN Access and Recovery
documents.section_path: IT Support / VPN Access and Recovery
documents.chunk_text: VPN is only required for admin networks; most SaaS tools work without it.

documents.title: VPN Access and Recovery
documents.section_path: IT Support / VPN Access and Recovery
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Open AnyConnect and connect to vpn.northwind.local. Approve the MFA push. If it fails, reinstall the profile from Self-Service.

documents.title: MFA Device Replacement
documents.section_path: IT Support / MFA Device Replacement
documents.chunk_text: Re-enrolling MFA after getting a new phone. You must verify identity with a manager video confirmation before MFA reset. Reset is performed by the Service Desk, not self-service, to prevent account takeover. After reset you have 24 hours to enroll the new device before access is suspended. Related terms: two-factor authentication, authenticator reset, new phone enrollment.

documents.title: MFA Device Replacement
documents.section_path: IT Support / MFA Device Replacement
documents.chunk_text: Hardware security keys are recommended for engineers with production access.

documents.title: MFA Device Replacement
documents.section_path: IT Support / MFA Device Replacement
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Open a Service Desk ticket for MFA reset. Complete the manager identity verification. Enroll your new device within 24 hours.

documents.title: Laptop Replacement and Repair
documents.section_path: IT Support / Laptop Replacement and Repair
documents.chunk_text: Getting a broken or lost laptop replaced. Standard replacement laptops ship within 2 business days from the local depot. A lost laptop must be reported within 1 hour so it can be remotely wiped. All laptops are encrypted, so a lost device does not expose data if reported. Related terms: computer replacement, broken machine, lost or stolen device.

documents.title: Laptop Replacement and Repair
documents.section_path: IT Support / Laptop Replacement and Repair
documents.chunk_text: Your data is recoverable if you used the synced Documents folder.

documents.title: Laptop Replacement and Repair
documents.section_path: IT Support / Laptop Replacement and Repair
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Report the issue to the Service Desk. For a lost device, trigger remote wipe immediately. Collect the replacement and restore from sync.

documents.title: Software Installation Requests
documents.section_path: IT Support / Software Installation Requests
documents.chunk_text: Installing software outside the standard image. Pre-approved software installs instantly from the Self-Service Portal. Non-standard software needs a license owner and a security review. Engineers can install developer tools from the approved Homebrew/winget list without a ticket. Related terms: application installation, program install request, why a downloaded app is blocked, unapproved software won't run, non-standard software restrictions.

documents.title: Software Installation Requests
documents.section_path: IT Support / Software Installation Requests
documents.chunk_text: Unapproved software is blocked by endpoint policy.

documents.title: Software Installation Requests
documents.section_path: IT Support / Software Installation Requests
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Check the Self-Service Portal first. For other software, open a request with a business reason. Provide license details for paid software.

documents.title: Email and Distribution Lists
documents.section_path: IT Support / Email and Distribution Lists
documents.chunk_text: Requesting shared mailboxes and distribution lists. Distribution lists follow the pattern team-<name>@northwind.local. Every list needs a named owner who approves membership. Shared mailboxes are reviewed annually for active owners. Related terms: mailing list, shared mailbox, group email, external email to an internal list, messages from outside the company, quarantined external mail.

documents.title: Email and Distribution Lists
documents.section_path: IT Support / Email and Distribution Lists
documents.chunk_text: External senders to internal lists are quarantined by default.

documents.title: Email and Distribution Lists
documents.section_path: IT Support / Email and Distribution Lists
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Request the list in the Access Portal with an owner. Define the membership rule. Confirm delivery with a test message.

documents.title: Paid Time Off Policy
documents.section_path: People and HR / Paid Time Off Policy
documents.chunk_text: How annual leave, carryover, and approval work. Full-time employees receive 25 days of annual leave per calendar year. Up to 5 unused days carry over and expire on 31 March of the next year. Leave is requested in the HR Portal and approved by the direct manager. Related terms: vacation days, annual leave, time off request, how far in advance to request leave, notice period for booking a vacation, planning time off ahead.

documents.title: Paid Time Off Policy
documents.section_path: People and HR / Paid Time Off Policy
documents.chunk_text: Requests of 5 or more consecutive days should be submitted 3 weeks in advance.

documents.title: Paid Time Off Policy
documents.section_path: People and HR / Paid Time Off Policy
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Submit the leave request in the HR Portal. Get manager approval. Arrange coverage for any on-call duty.

documents.title: Remote and Hybrid Work
documents.section_path: People and HR / Remote and Hybrid Work
documents.chunk_text: Expectations for where and when employees work. The default is hybrid: at least 2 days per week in the office for office-assigned roles. Fully remote arrangements require VP approval and a signed remote work agreement. Core collaboration hours are 10:00 to 16:00 in the employee's home time zone. Related terms: work from home, hybrid schedule, working abroad.

documents.title: Remote and Hybrid Work
documents.section_path: People and HR / Remote and Hybrid Work
documents.chunk_text: Working from abroad over 30 days requires a tax and security review.

documents.title: Remote and Hybrid Work
documents.section_path: People and HR / Remote and Hybrid Work
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Agree your pattern with your manager. For fully remote, request VP approval. For long stays abroad, start the tax review early.

documents.title: On-Call Compensation
documents.section_path: People and HR / On-Call Compensation
documents.chunk_text: How engineers are compensated for on-call duty. On-call is one week at a time and rotates so no one does more than one week per month. On-call carries a flat weekly stipend plus time off in lieu for night pages. Being paged after midnight grants a late start the next day. Related terms: on-call pay, duty stipend, after-hours compensation.

documents.title: On-Call Compensation
documents.section_path: People and HR / On-Call Compensation
documents.chunk_text: On-call eligibility starts only after on-call shadowing is complete.

documents.title: On-Call Compensation
documents.section_path: People and HR / On-Call Compensation
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Confirm the rotation in the schedule. Record any night pages for time in lieu. Hand off cleanly at the end of the week.

documents.title: Performance Review Cycle
documents.section_path: People and HR / Performance Review Cycle
documents.chunk_text: Timing and inputs for performance reviews. Reviews run twice a year, in June and December. Each review includes a self-assessment and at least two peer reviews. Calibration happens at the department level before ratings are shared. Related terms: appraisal, evaluation cycle, promotion process.

documents.title: Performance Review Cycle
documents.section_path: People and HR / Performance Review Cycle
documents.chunk_text: Promotion cases are submitted during the December cycle.

documents.title: Performance Review Cycle
documents.section_path: People and HR / Performance Review Cycle
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Write your self-assessment by the deadline. Nominate peer reviewers. Discuss outcomes in your 1:1.

documents.title: Health Insurance and Benefits Enrollment
documents.section_path: People and HR / Health Insurance and Benefits Enrollment
documents.chunk_text: Enrolling in health insurance and core benefits. New hires have 30 days from their start date to enroll in health insurance. The company covers 100% of the employee premium and 60% for dependents. Open enrollment for changes runs every November. Related terms: medical coverage, health benefits, insurance enrollment.

documents.title: Health Insurance and Benefits Enrollment
documents.section_path: People and HR / Health Insurance and Benefits Enrollment
documents.chunk_text: A qualifying life event allows changes outside open enrollment within 30 days.

documents.title: Health Insurance and Benefits Enrollment
documents.section_path: People and HR / Health Insurance and Benefits Enrollment
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Open the Benefits Portal within 30 days of starting. Select your plan and add dependents. Upload any required dependent proof.

documents.title: Learning and Development Budget
documents.section_path: People and HR / Learning and Development Budget
documents.chunk_text: Using the annual training budget. Each engineer has an annual learning budget of 1,500 EUR. Conferences, courses, books, and certifications are eligible. Manager approval is required before booking; reimbursement needs a receipt. Related terms: training budget, education allowance, conference funding, unused learning budget, does the education allowance carry over, training budget expiry.

documents.title: Learning and Development Budget
documents.section_path: People and HR / Learning and Development Budget
documents.chunk_text: Unused budget does not carry over to the next year.

documents.title: Learning and Development Budget
documents.section_path: People and HR / Learning and Development Budget
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Pick an eligible course or conference. Get manager approval in the HR Portal. Submit the receipt for reimbursement.

documents.title: Expense Reimbursement
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Expense Reimbursement
documents.chunk_text: How to claim work-related expenses. Expenses are submitted in the Expense Tool within 60 days of the purchase. Receipts are mandatory for any expense over 25 EUR. Approved expenses are paid with the next monthly payroll run. Related terms: reimbursement claim, business expense report, are alcohol expenses reimbursed, what costs are not covered, non-reimbursable expenses.

documents.title: Expense Reimbursement
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Expense Reimbursement
documents.chunk_text: Alcohol and personal items are not reimbursable.

documents.title: Expense Reimbursement
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Expense Reimbursement
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Photograph the receipt and create an expense. Assign the correct cost center. Submit for manager approval.

documents.title: Software and SaaS Purchasing
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Software and SaaS Purchasing
documents.chunk_text: Buying new software tools and subscriptions. Any new SaaS tool requires a security and data-privacy review before purchase. Purchases over 5,000 EUR per year need finance director approval. Prefer existing approved vendors before evaluating new ones. Related terms: software procurement, tool purchase approval, vendor buying.

documents.title: Software and SaaS Purchasing
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Software and SaaS Purchasing
documents.chunk_text: Annual contracts are preferred over monthly for tools used by whole teams.

documents.title: Software and SaaS Purchasing
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Software and SaaS Purchasing
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Check the approved vendor list first. Submit a purchase request with a business case. Complete the security review for new vendors.

documents.title: Cloud Cost Management
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Cloud Cost Management
documents.chunk_text: How teams stay accountable for cloud spend. Every cloud resource must carry team, service, and environment tags. Untagged resources are flagged daily and may be stopped after 7 days. Each team gets a monthly cost report and a budget alert at 80% of plan. Related terms: cloud spend, infrastructure cost control, resource tagging, scaling down dev environments after hours, non-production resources outside working hours, overnight shutdown of dev environments.

documents.title: Cloud Cost Management
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Cloud Cost Management
documents.chunk_text: Non-production environments scale down outside working hours.

documents.title: Cloud Cost Management
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Cloud Cost Management
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Tag all resources at creation. Review the monthly cost report with your team. Investigate any spend anomaly alert.

documents.title: Vendor Invoice Processing
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Vendor Invoice Processing
documents.chunk_text: How supplier invoices are received and paid. Invoices are emailed to invoices@northwind.local or uploaded to the Invoice Portal. An invoice must match an approved purchase order to be paid. Standard payment terms are net 30 from invoice date. Related terms: supplier billing, accounts payable, invoice payment.

documents.title: Vendor Invoice Processing
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Vendor Invoice Processing
documents.chunk_text: Disputed invoices are put on hold and the supplier is notified within 5 days.

documents.title: Vendor Invoice Processing
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Vendor Invoice Processing
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Match the invoice to its purchase order. Confirm goods or services were received. Approve for payment or raise a dispute.

documents.title: Team Budget and Forecast
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Team Budget and Forecast
documents.chunk_text: How engineering managers track their budget. Budgets are set annually and reviewed each quarter. Headcount is the largest line and is forecast in the Planning Tool. A variance over 10% against plan requires a written explanation. Related terms: department finances, budget planning, forecasting, capital versus operating expense, how equipment purchases are accounted, asset depreciation.

documents.title: Team Budget and Forecast
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Team Budget and Forecast
documents.chunk_text: Capital purchases are depreciated and tracked separately from operating cost.

documents.title: Team Budget and Forecast
documents.section_path: Finance and Procurement / Team Budget and Forecast
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Review the monthly actuals against plan. Update the forecast in the Planning Tool. Explain variances over 10%.

documents.title: Data Classification Policy
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Data Classification Policy
documents.chunk_text: The four data classes and how to handle each. Data is classified as Public, Internal, Confidential, or Restricted. Customer personal data is always at least Confidential. Restricted data, such as payment card data, may never be stored in logs or screenshots. Related terms: data sensitivity levels, information handling rules, default handling of customer personal data, how to treat PII, personal information classification.

documents.title: Data Classification Policy
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Data Classification Policy
documents.chunk_text: Confidential data must be encrypted at rest and in transit.

documents.title: Data Classification Policy
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Data Classification Policy
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Determine the highest class of data you handle. Apply the matching storage and sharing rules. Ask Security if you are unsure.

documents.title: Security Incident Response
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Security Incident Response
documents.chunk_text: What to do when you suspect a security incident. Report suspected incidents within 30 minutes to security@northwind.local or #security-incident. Do not investigate alone; the on-call security engineer coordinates response. Preserve evidence: do not delete logs or wipe affected machines. Related terms: security breach procedure, compromised account response.

documents.title: Security Incident Response
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Security Incident Response
documents.chunk_text: Customer-impacting breaches trigger the legal and communications playbook.

documents.title: Security Incident Response
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Security Incident Response
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Report immediately through the security channel. Preserve all evidence. Follow the incident commander's instructions.

documents.title: Access Reviews
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Access Reviews
documents.chunk_text: Quarterly review of who can access what. Access to production and customer data is reviewed every quarter. Managers must confirm or revoke each team member's access within 10 business days. Access not confirmed in time is automatically revoked. Related terms: permission audit, quarterly access certification, auditing service and machine accounts, non-human account reviews, bot account access review.

documents.title: Access Reviews
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Access Reviews
documents.chunk_text: Service accounts are reviewed at the same cadence as people.

documents.title: Access Reviews
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Access Reviews
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Open the access review task when notified. Confirm or revoke each entry. Complete within 10 business days.

documents.title: Secure Coding Guidelines
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Secure Coding Guidelines
documents.chunk_text: Baseline practices to avoid common vulnerabilities. Never build SQL with string concatenation; use parameterized queries. All input crossing a trust boundary is validated and encoded on output. Dependencies are scanned on every PR and high-severity findings block merge. Related terms: application security practices, vulnerability prevention, injection protection.

documents.title: Secure Coding Guidelines
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Secure Coding Guidelines
documents.chunk_text: Secrets are read from the vault, never hardcoded or committed.

documents.title: Secure Coding Guidelines
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Secure Coding Guidelines
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Use parameterized queries and safe APIs. Validate and encode untrusted input. Resolve high-severity dependency alerts before merge.

documents.title: Vendor Risk and SOC 2 Evidence
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Vendor Risk and SOC 2 Evidence
documents.chunk_text: How vendor reviews and audit evidence are organized. New vendors handling customer data must provide a current SOC 2 Type II report. Evidence for our own SOC 2 audit is collected continuously, not at year end. Each control has a named owner and a quarterly evidence refresh. Related terms: supplier security assessment, audit evidence, compliance reports, external auditor access level, what auditors are allowed to see, read-only access for auditors.

documents.title: Vendor Risk and SOC 2 Evidence
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Vendor Risk and SOC 2 Evidence
documents.chunk_text: Auditor access is read-only and time-boxed to the audit window.

documents.title: Vendor Risk and SOC 2 Evidence
documents.section_path: Security and Compliance / Vendor Risk and SOC 2 Evidence
documents.chunk_text: How this is done: Request the vendor's SOC 2 report. File it in the vendor risk register. Refresh control evidence each quarter.

documents.title: Attachment: workstation checklist
documents.section_path: Onboarding / workstation checklist
documents.chunk_text: Workstation Setup Checklist _Attached to the "Developer Workstation Setup" onboarding guide in the Onboarding section, owned by Developer Platform._ Copy-paste checklist to confirm a workstation is ready. - Git installed and commit signing verified - Docker Desktop running with 8 GB memory allocated - Node.js 20 LTS and Python 3.12 on PATH - Authenticated to registry.northwind.local - `nw doctor` reports all green

documents.title: Attachment: coding standards
documents.section_path: Engineering Handbook / coding standards
documents.chunk_text: Coding Standards _Attached to the "Coding Standards and Formatting" engineering standard in the Engineering Handbook section, owned by Architecture Office._ The normative coding standards enforced across repositories. Python - Format with black, lint with ruff - Type-check with mypy --strict - Max line length 100 TypeScript - Format with Prettier, lint with ESLint - tsc --strict must pass - Prefer named exports General - Public APIs are documented - No commented-out code in main - Tests live next to the code

documents.title: Attachment: billing service readme
documents.section_path: Project Setups / billing service readme
documents.chunk_text: billing-service README _Attached to the "Billing Service Setup" project setup record in the Project Setups section, owned by Payments Team._ Quick reference for running billing-service locally. Ports - API: 8020 - Postgres: 5432 Key commands - alembic upgrade head - pytest tests/smoke - uvicorn billing.main:app --port 8020 Gotchas - Stripe webhooks need the CLI: stripe listen --forward-to localhost:8020/webhooks

documents.title: Attachment: api latency runbook
documents.section_path: Runbooks and Troubleshooting / api latency runbook
documents.chunk_text: API Latency Runbook _Attached to the "High API Latency Runbook" runbook in the Runbooks and Troubleshooting section, owned by Platform Team._ Condensed steps for paging engineers at 3am. Alert - p95 > 800ms for 5 min First checks - API Overview dashboard - DB pool saturation - Recent deploy in releases Mitigations - Scale api-gateway to 6 replicas - Roll back last deploy if it correlates
